mcqs.question: What is the main difference between Procedural and OOP?

options.text: Procedural focuses on objects, while OOP focuses on procedures
Procedural focuses on procedures, while OOP focuses on objects
Procedural is top-down, while OOP is bottom-up
Procedural is object-oriented, while OOP is procedural

mcqs.explanation: Procedural programming focuses on procedures or functions, while OOP focuses on objects and their interactions

mcqs.question: What is a class in OOP?

options.text: An instance of an object
A blueprint or template for creating objects
A procedure or function
A variable or data type

mcqs.explanation: A class is a blueprint or template for creating objects in OOP

mcqs.question: What is inheritance in OOP?

options.text: The process of creating a new object based on an existing object
The process of creating a new class based on an existing class
The process of creating a new procedure or function
The process of creating a new variable or data type

mcqs.explanation: Inheritance is the process of creating a new class based on an existing class in OOP

mcqs.question: What is polymorphism in OOP?

options.text: The ability of an object to take on multiple forms
The ability of a procedure or function to perform multiple tasks
The ability of a variable or data type to hold multiple values
The ability of a class to create multiple objects

mcqs.explanation: Polymorphism is the ability of an object to take on multiple forms in OOP

mcqs.question: What is encapsulation in OOP?

options.text: The practice of hiding an object's internal details and exposing only its necessary information
The practice of exposing an object's internal details and hiding only its necessary information
The practice of creating a new object based on an existing object
The practice of creating a new class based on an existing class

mcqs.explanation: Encapsulation is the practice of hiding an object's internal details and exposing only its necessary information in OOP

mcqs.question: What is the advantage of using OOP?

options.text: It is easy to learn and use
It is efficient and suitable for small programs
It promotes code reuse and is easy to maintain
It is difficult to learn and use

mcqs.explanation: OOP promotes code reuse and is easy to maintain, making it a popular choice for large and complex programs

mcqs.question: What is the disadvantage of using Procedural Programming?

options.text: It is easy to learn and use
It is efficient and suitable for small programs
It is difficult to maintain and modify
It is not suitable for large programs

mcqs.explanation: Procedural Programming can be difficult to maintain and modify, especially for large and complex programs

mcqs.question: What is the main difference between a class and an object in OOP?

options.text: A class is an instance of an object
An object is an instance of a class
A class is a blueprint for creating objects
An object is a blueprint for creating classes

mcqs.explanation: An object is an instance of a class in OOP

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of inheritance in OOP?

options.text: To create a new object based on an existing object
To create a new class based on an existing class
To create a new procedure or function
To create a new variable or data type

mcqs.explanation: The purpose of inheritance is to create a new class based on an existing class in OOP

mcqs.question: What is the benefit of using polymorphism in OOP?

options.text: It allows for more efficient code
It allows for more flexible code
It allows for more reusable code
It allows for more complex code

mcqs.explanation: Polymorphism allows for more flexible code in OOP

options.text: To hide an object's internal details and expose only its necessary information
To expose an object's internal details and hide only its necessary information
To create a new object based on an existing object
To create a new class based on an existing class
